rationale.R1.1: The section's contribution claim ('consistently improves across all task types') is directly refuted by the artifact's own data — medical accuracy fell and legal showed no effect — so the thesis framing requires reconstruction regardless of which sub-task is prioritized.
rationale.R1.2: The concluding paragraph's generalizations are logically inconsistent with the per-domain results: a 9-point accuracy drop in medical and two non-significant domains cannot jointly support 'consistent direction of effects.' This cross-section incoherence is load-bearing if any substantive help is intended.

Evidence-claim misalignment here is not marginal — it inverts the data for two of four domains.
